transcript.text: What's up?
I'm guessing I can remove one of these GrowthX note-takers.
Yeah, you can get rid of the, I don't know why Adio is joining this.
That's actually weird.
All right.
Yeah, that one's getting removed.
Cool.
I know how you love multiple note-takers.
hate them.
All right.
All right.
All right.
Why is it so cold?
Hold on this right now.
Jesus.
You need a link to the Panda doc?
Just like, so it's Andy?
Yeah, I got it.
Yeah.
And I guess the big thing is like, we've got like the SVP of marketing's approval, so we can ideally like, be a bit more assertive with Andy.
We obviously be respectful, but theoretically we should have approval, so it shouldn't be much of a need to, right, like, do much more than the discount that we talked about.
Cool.
All right, I'll let you lead unless you need anything from me.
He's in the waiting room, and I'll bring him in once you give me a thumbs up.
Yeah, let's do it.
All right, let's do it.
Making it happen.
Cool.
We'll be right back.
Hi.
Hey, Andy.
There we go.
Hi, guys.
There we are.
Hey, man, how's it going?
Good.
I am right outside the QBR.
Well, appreciate you jumping out.
didn't realize we were catching you on a crazy day.
So, yeah, I just wanted to follow up on, you know, the email you had sent before around the negotiation and just wanted to make sure.
You know, we can make sure you're all feeling good about the partnership.
We're super excited about it.
We sent some details to Megan earlier just via text via Marcel.
I'm not sure if you got any of that through her, if you're sitting close by during the QBR.
But, yeah, no worries on going off video.
But let me just pull up the existing agreement.
So we're just anchoring on the right stuff here.
All right.
You should be able to see my screen,
Yep, so there's a couple things I just wanted to clarify before I go into some of the things around, like, cost.
For one, you had mentioned platform setup fee or something to that effect in your email.
I just want to make sure we're on the same page of, like, what we're really offering here, and maybe you've picked up on some of the cents.
But we're not looking at this as a setup fee.
Really, you know, we are a services play.
There is a platform aspect to what we do, but that is largely for our team.
so, largely, this is a helping build the strategy, executing on the content, helping stage and publish it, and owning that end-to-end for your team.
And so, we see a ton of surface area and opportunity there in talking with Megan.
And so, that's part of what informed the pricing that we have.
Essentially, the way we structure this is in the strategy sprint phase.
We are laying a lot of the critical foundation, you know, to really dialing in.
And how do we move really, really fast in generating content and hitting a repeatable bar of quality?

So there's a lot of things we've learned in terms of development, a lot of rich artifacts, context engineering, starting to really dial in the process of how we're setting up the roadmap, starting to calibrate on the content that aligns to that roadmap.

And so a lot of what we're doing during the strategy sprint phase is starting to develop that operational muscle and making things more repeatable, as well as defining what is needed to reach the ultimate objectives from a long-term standpoint in this growth execution phase.
And so we look at this as like a low-risk way to, you know, understand how we work, what the strategy is, help to build artifacts that even can be used outside of the strategy sprint.
We found a lot of teams are leveraging this for other things they're doing, getting a lot of valuable outputs.
So you'll have the strategy.
You'll also have, we're producing content in this period of time, be clear.
It's not just pure strategy.
You know, we've had several accounts by.
You know, this is an eight-week sprint, but, you know, by, like, week five or so, like, we published 20-plus pieces of content.
This isn't just pure strategy.

It's about, again, how do we build that operational muscle and making sure that we feel really good that we're hitting that right bar and going through all that necessary back and forth to establish that.
At the end of this...
And what are these artifacts?
Yeah, so we had a chance to...
you said that, you know, some of your other customers...
Yeah, you said some of your...
Yeah, sorry.
I was saying that you said that some of your customers typically end up using that, the artifacts in their day-to-day beyond just the content gen.
So what are you producing during that time?
Yeah, so we've gone through this really deep with your team, you know, with quite a few folks.
And so, you know, happy to walk you through that.
But my intention today was not to go through and pitch and make sure we're going through and understanding the contract details.
You know, from my perspective...
sure The
Teams like Megan and team are pretty bought in, and I just want to make sure that we're aligned on, you know, the terms, the key details to moving forward.
And so largely just want to close the loop and make sure you understand that.

But think of this as these artifacts are leveraged both in terms of helping us extract knowledge from your team, help educate our team, but also then plugged into our AI workflows that essentially are feeding in all that rich context that goes into some of that context engineering I was talking about.

So think of it as context about your company, your personas, what makes them tick, what motivates them, what are potential things we need to stay away from, rich writing guidelines, what are some really good examples, bad examples of the things like voice, tone, structure, you know, all this is essentially the things that help us avoid playing whack-a-mole with each piece of content and doing...
Excessive line edits.
And so a big part of that is setting these artifacts early on with your team.
So just to understand, this is, you're saying there's no flexibility in the pricing here.
Is that right?
No, no.
I just want to make sure we're understanding first and foremost, like, what is the strategy sprint?
One, another piece I haven't been able to get to here yet is there also is a mutual opt-out at the end of this period.
We want to ensure that both sides have high conviction on the long-term engagement.
And so what we found is this is a really successful way for us to lay that foundation and for brands to feel really strong about what a strategy would look like in working with us.
So here is a couple of things that we sent to to Megan earlier, understanding you all want to try to explore flexibility.
You know, where we can go on the strategy sprint is down to $30,000.

That's quite a bit low for us in terms of complexity that we see here, but we really believe that there's just such a massive opportunity for a long-term partnership here, and one that we have high conviction we can crush it on.
So, you know, for us, you know, taking off that $10,000 at the strategy sprint is something I can definitely get buy-in from our CEO on.
Related to the execution phase, here we priced it at $30,000.

This is typically something that, you know, is about our starting place that we see for a lot of brands in terms of, like, how much surface area and refresh of content as well as net new content that's going to be needed.
But just to, you know, ensure you're feeling strong as well, like, I got, I did talk to Marcel, our CEO, about bringing this down to $25,000.
And so that would essentially be $10,000 off the strategy sprint, $5,000 off of the growth.
Execution.
And, you know, essentially, like, that's us, you know, doing that in a, from a perspective of, hey, if we can move fast here, and, you know, all sides are feeling good, I can get buy-in on that cost.
You know, for us, part of what we're wanting to, you know, you know, why speed is a value for us is just full transparency.
We are fundraising in October.
And, you know, we're our big users of Redis here.
We're huge fans as well.
And anything we can do to kind of strengthen that partnership and show this momentum, you know, is where I can get flexibility on things like price.
Yeah, I can talk to our sales team, too, on, like, what's going on, see if there's added opportunity here for something mutually beneficial.
But, you know, one thing that did concern me is
Because the contract, mean, like I was explaining, right?
This is still a pretty steep price.
Regardless, like, let's just look at the price tag in isolation, right?
It is still a very expensive line item in the budget.

would, like, even if you knock it down to $25,000 a month, it would still be over 30% of the total marketing team SaaS budget, which is kind of wild because it would still be more than one and a half times what we're paying for things like Marketo.
It's, you know, which is basically the sub that most of the other tools float want to categorize this as SaaS budget, to be clear.
I mean, most brands we talk to, we're not in the SaaS line item.
But, you know, understand that might be different brand to brand.
But, yeah, sorry.
Just wanted to jump in and make sure that the services angle is clear.
I mean, okay, so from a services perspective, it would be the most expensive service then.
Right.
So there's definitely like, regardless of what you categorize it under, the point is that it is a very expensive line item.
And what I'm not clear on looking at the SoW is that what are the returns on it?
Because what I do see is that the terms, the SoW talks about how many content pieces you're going to generate each week.
On average, I think it said targeting 10 a week, if I remember correctly.
But what I'm not following is that it's like, at that cost, you want to see an ROI also.
And so what kind of ROI should we expect from this?
Are we expecting organic traffic to jump up 20%, 50%, 100%?
Should we expect different, better conversions on the website?
Like, what are we paying for other than just volume of content?
Yeah, mean, certainly any brand we work with, the intent is going to be to drive engagement, conversion, ultimately revenue through...
You the programs that we're running, you know, I won't be able to bake that directly into the contract.
That's just something that we don't do, but certainly can explore, you know, how to look at varying opsizing of the opportunity that's out there.
You know, certainly understand the perspective of wanting to, you know, get a clearer view of the ROI.
You know, the way we typically approach that is looking at, you know, assumptions of, you know, how much headroom do you have in terms of traffic?
What are some relatively realistic versions of timelines to be able to realize some of that headroom?
And then we can work with your team to bake in assumptions on conversion rates.
You know, at the same time, I just want to make sure, you know, that's going to be something that's going to be useful and is going to help with the decision.
So, yeah.
And, you know, we can certainly take that back and explore if helpful.
But, yeah, you know, in terms of like, again, just to make sure it's clear, like baking an ROI into the contract, that's just something we have never done with any brands.
But we can certainly explore what that looks like outside and making sure you have a perspective.
Yeah, think at the very least a perspective would be good because right now, you know, we're walking into it blind.
And if you would rather we talk to a referral customer or something, we can do that.
But, like, I mean, I can talk to the team as ultimately Megan's budget, not mine.
But if she's basically like, yeah, turn off digital ad spend for a quarter so that way we can get these 500 content pieces up.
And, you know, I have strong conviction that it's going to drive up more organic traffic than we were getting from.
And that's, you know, that's her call.
But I think getting an understanding of like, what is the trade-off?
Because that 300K to, you know, 300, 330K isn't going to materialize out of nowhere.
So I need to be able to explain the opportunity cost and why this is better.
To my understanding, talking to Megan live when she came in, that it seemed that it had already been carved out, but perhaps some things have changed.
But that was at least what she shared live with Marcel and myself.
Andy, have you had a chance to connect with Megan about the GrowthX partnership specifically?
I have.
And it was basically like, we don't have budget.
So we need to figure out, one, what are we paying for?
What are we getting out of it?
How much can we drive the price down?
And what we turn off in complements to funding this.
It, as you all had talked about that, just to make sure we're anchoring.
Was there a more realistic expectation that you all had in mind where, you know, this becomes an easier equation for you?
I can't obviously promise that that's going to be a fit for us, but I just want to make sure, like, how far are we from that reality that you all had in your mind?
Sorry, you cut off of the first half, so I missed what you were saying.
Oh, shoot.
I think it's my internet, but yeah.
no worries.
Man, pressure, can I say it best as well as I did the first time?

Largely what I was getting at was, as you all were thinking about, you know, that price point that was closer to your expectation where this becomes an easier, you know, equation to sort through in your minds, do you have any perspective you can share around that?
I can't promise we can get to that a little bit, but I'm just trying to understand, like, how far apart, you know, when you say drive the price down, you know, where you all were thinking.
down, know, know, you know, when the
Yeah, again, I think a lot of it is just understanding the expected returns, right?
Because our total budget exceeds what GrowthX is quoting here.
But what I'm trying to understand is that would we be better off doing a smaller engagement with you and keeping everything else on?
Or would we actually be better off turning off other things and doubling down and tripling down on GrowthX?
So that's why I'm trying to get some understanding of what are we actually going to get from this, whether it's $200, $300, $400, whatever K we spend on this.
But if it is going to be better performance than digital marketing, then it's an easy conversation to have, even if it is a $400K price tag.
But if we can't do that, then I have to figure out, okay, what is our risk tolerance here?
And do we want to still turn off digital and just...
Cross our fingers, cross our toes, that in 12 months we don't tank the business or do we negotiate a lower price?
Yeah, I get you.
No, I mean, it makes total sense and I would be thinking about it quite similarly and have in the past many times before.
So I totally understand that.
Cool.
Is there anything in addition we should talk about that was a point of ambiguity or concern?

You know, think what you're mentioning here around getting a perspective on ROI is quite clear and, you know, we can take that as a follow-up, but I want to make sure if there's anything else we can address, we're chatting about that as well.
I think the SoW is pretty straightforward.
It's just the ROI side of it that I'm not following.
And then I think once I have that, then I can also come back to you with, like, what we're comfortable with cost-wise because I'll be able to get...
And an estimate of the total impact of the business then, right?
Because I can say that, okay, if we're expecting a 10, like I was saying, right, a 10% lift in organic, then to me, like, that could be worth X thousand dollars in digital.
And therefore, I can take that X thousand out and put it towards growth place, right?
So I can do that math and then tell you, like, hey, this is based on the math of what I think I can roughly afford.

And then we can have that back and forth of, like, what you can provide for that price or if you can make that price work and we'll, you know, make it up to you in other ways through testimonials and case studies and all that for your fundraising ground.
Yep.
Yep.
Totally understand.
Cool.
We can take that back.
And, you know, to the point you mentioned earlier related to any sort of references, you know, we're also happy to help with that as well.
And connect you with folks that help you all just feel more comfortable and confident moving forward.
So it will probably take us.
It's a couple of days just to bring some of that data together, but we can aim to get something back over to you for Friday.

And, you know, again, what that will likely look like is just a bit of like, this is a bit of the, what the, you know, scenarios of what is possible and then the, you know, quarterly breakdown of like when we think we'll hit varying milestones.
I'm happy to tweak as necessary.
You know, we can debate inputs and things of that nature, but at least we'll make sure the logic is clear for you.
Cool.
That would be super helpful.
And if in parallel, sorry, Tyler, just wanted to And if in parallel you want to connect us to a customer referral, then we can do that too.
So that way I can get the anecdotal feedback while you also work on the formula.
Yeah.
Cool.
Cool.
Sorry, Tyler, what were you saying?
Yeah.
The one thing I think we haven't covered is just making sure we're on the same page about the timeline for procurement.
Once we reach alignment, the reason I asked.
Thank
It's because right now we have a kickoff date of August 25th, and we are limiting the number of kickoffs we do each week.
So I just want to make sure that the kickoff date is feasible at the end of the day.
Any perspective there?
Based on probably like the dozens of contracts I've handled here, I don't think I've seen a single one get processed in like 5, 10 business days.
So I think realistically that August 25th is, let's not plan for that to begin with.

I think it would probably take three full business weeks start to finish because most likely unless you come down drastically in the price, it will have, it will get escalated really high up in the chain for approval.
So which will obviously come with its own, you know, blocking and tackling we'll have to do on our side.
Yep.
Makes sense.
And just want to make sure that Kyle's point about, you know, what you're signing here is a commitment to the strategy sprint and you have the ability to opt out of the growth execution.

So I guess before we like do too much on both sides, wanted to see if like we needed to get things super specific before we can initiate procurement or if we would have the ability to initiate procurement, you know, with the understanding that the growth execution is something we can continue to talk about during the strategy sprint.
And you guys would have the ability to opt out if you, right, didn't have confidence in what the next 12 months would hold.
I have not seen that get much success here.
Okay.
So I would say let's just move forward with getting more of this stuff figured out and then we'll start all this.
think what we can probably begin with right now is the NDAs, MSAs, those kind of, that kind of paperwork.
I think in terms of like actually sending it in for signature and all that and, you know, doing a part two negotiation, I don't see that happening here, unfortunately.
Yeah, no problem.
I think if you could send us over any of the NDA or MSA documentation, that would be helpful for us to start with.
And Kyle, we'll work together to make sure that you have some more clarity on the ROI or a potential customer reference in parallel, if that helps accelerate.
Yep, for sure.
I'll do that.
Cool.
Awesome.
Well, appreciate you jumping out of the QBR, Andy.
Hopefully you didn't miss anything too critical.
But we'll get back to you shortly.
Sounds good.
Thank you both.
All right.
Take care.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Negotiate partnership terms and pricing between GrowthX and Redis, addressing concerns and clarifying ROI expectations.](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=141.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Redis seeks clearer ROI projections and potentially lower pricing for GrowthX's content strategy and execution services](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
  - [GrowthX offered a reduced price of $30k for strategy sprint and $25k/month for execution phase](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [Andy (Redis) expressed concerns about the high cost relative to their marketing budget and requested more concrete ROI estimates](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)
  - [GrowthX will provide ROI projections and customer references to help Redis evaluate the partnership's potential value](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)

## Topics

### Pricing and Contract Structure

  - [GrowthX initially offered $40k for strategy sprint, $30k/month for execution](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [Reduced offer: $30k for strategy sprint, $25k/month for execution](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=534.0)
  - [Total contract value: \~$330k for strategy sprint + 12 months execution](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [Redis concerned about high cost (\>30% of total marketing SaaS budget)](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=615.0)
  - [GrowthX emphasized this is a services engagement, not a SaaS product](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)

### ROI Expectations and Justification

  - [Redis requested clear ROI projections (e.g., organic traffic increase, conversion improvements)](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
  - [GrowthX unable to contractually guarantee specific ROI figures](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=786.0)
  - [Andy seeking data to justify potential trade-offs (e.g., reducing digital ad spend)](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=982.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide ROI scenarios and quarterly milestone projections by Friday](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)

### Content Production and Strategy

  - [Strategy sprint (8 weeks) aims to build operational muscle and establish content quality bar](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)
  - [Targeting \~10 content pieces per week during execution phase](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Includes context engineering, persona development, and writing guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=372.0)

### Procurement and Timeline

  - [Original kickoff date of August 25th likely unrealistic](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1233.0)
  - [Redis estimates 3 full business weeks for contract processing](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1233.0)
  - [High-level approval likely needed due to contract value](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1239.0)
  - [GrowthX's suggestion of partial commitment (strategy sprint only) not feasible for Redis](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1301.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to provide ROI projections and scenarios by Friday](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [GrowthX to connect Redis with customer references for anecdotal feedback](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1124.0)
  - [Redis to send over NDA and MSA documentation to start paperwork process](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1312.0)
  - [Both parties to continue negotiations based on ROI projections and reference feedback](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1349.0)
  - [Adjust timeline expectations, moving away from August 25th kickoff date](https://fathom.video/share/oLNmap1aZy7aLvSP33xvxEn7Ha5VGxV6?tab=summary&timestamp=1233.0)

action_items.description: Identify and connect Andy with a customer reference for GrowthX services
Prepare ROI estimates for Redis (scenarios, quarterly breakdown, milestones) by Friday
Send NDA and MSA documentation to GrowthX team for review
